HomePage.title: Headerly
HomePage.description: Instantly view and inspect your HTTP request headers in a clean, developer-friendly interface.

privacy.title: Privacy First
privacy.description: Headers are processed server-side and never stored. What you see is only visible to you and stays within your session.

devTools.title: Developer Tools
devTools.description: Built for developers. Copy individual values or the entire JSON payload with a single click. High performance and easy to use.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: Headerly is open-source and MIT licensed. Explore the code on GitHub, report issues, or contribute to make it better.

NetworkPage.title: Network Details
NetworkPage.description: Explore the technical blueprint of your current connection, derived solely from request metadata.

howItWorks.title: How is this data provided?
howItWorks.subtitle: Privacy-first, transparent, and secure.
howItWorks.p1: At Headerly, your privacy is our top priority. We do not rely on third-party geolocation APIs or share your usage data.
howItWorks.p2: All the network and location details displayed above are extracted solely from the HTTP request metadata sent by your browser. We use secure edge computing headers (Cloudflare Edge) to determine your approximate origin in real-time.

noTracking.title: Zero Third-Party Tracking
noTracking.description: No external APIs ever see your IP address.

realTime.title: Real-Time Processing
realTime.description: Data is analyzed on-the-fly at the network edge.

nonPersistent.title: Non-Persistent Data
nonPersistent.description: We never store your connection details.

security.title: Edge-to-Edge Security
security.description: Encrypted delivery of your network profile.

UserAgentPage.title: User-Agent Analysis
UserAgentPage.description: Deep dive into your browser's digital fingerprint and understand how identification works.

howItWorks.title: How is this detected?
howItWorks.subtitle: Decoding the browser's identity string.
howItWorks.p1: Every time you visit a website, your browser sends a User-Agent header. This string acts as a "passport" that tells the server:

list.engine: Browser Engine: The underlying technology (like Blink for Chrome/Edge or Gecko for Firefox).
list.os: OS Details: The operating system and version you are running.
list.device: Device Info: Whether you are on a phone, tablet, or desktop, and sometimes the specific model.

howItWorks.p2: We use an advanced parsing library to break down this complex string into human-readable components. This process is done entirely on the server and the result is only used to display this dashboard.

matching.title: Pattern Matching
matching.description: We scan the string for specific keywords and version numbers used by vendors.

engine.title: Engine Inference
engine.description: Identifying the core rendering engine based on standard naming conventions.

privacy.title: Privacy Protected
privacy.description: Your ID string is processed in real-time and never saved or tracked.

realTime.title: Real-Time Analysis
realTime.description: Parsing happens in milliseconds during the request lifecycle.

GreenWebPage.title: Green Web Analyzer
GreenWebPage.description: Check if a website is hosted on green energy and estimate its carbon footprint.
GreenWebPage.error: Failed to analyze the website. Please check the URL and try again.

howItWorks.title: How is this calculated?
howItWorks.subtitle: Estimating the digital footprint of a webpage.
howItWorks.p1: Every byte transferred over the internet consumes energy. We estimate the webpage size, then check its domain against The Green Web Foundation's database to verify if it's powered by renewable energy.
howItWorks.p2: Using these two factors, we calculate an estimated CO2 emission per visit, demonstrating how website performance directly impacts environmental sustainability.

energy.title: Energy Consumption
energy.description: The internet is responsible for ~4% of global greenhouse gas emissions.

hosting.title: Green Hosting
hosting.description: Running data centers on renewable energy drastically reduces footprints.

performance.title: Performance = Ecology
performance.description: Optimized, lightweight websites require less energy to transfer and render.

privacy.title: Privacy Focused
privacy.description: Our stateless analysis logs no personal or request data.

HeaderCard.title: Request Headers
HeaderCard.description: Detecting active browser and connection headers

local.title: Local Environment Detected
local.description: You are accessing Headerly from a local or private network. Geolocation data is only available for public IP ranges.

browser.label: Browser
browser.description: The software you're using to browse

os.label: Operating System
os.description: The core system software of your device

engine.label: Engine
engine.description: The rendering engine of your browser

raw.title: Raw User-Agent String
raw.description: This is the full identification string sent by your browser.

Headers.accept: The Accept request header advertises which content types, expressed as MIME types, the client is able to understand.
Headers.accept-encoding: The Accept-Encoding request header advertises which content encoding, usually a compression algorithm, the client is able to understand.
Headers.accept-language: The Accept-Language request header advertises which languages the client is able to understand, and which locale variant is preferred.
Headers.authorization: The HTTP Authorization request header contains the credentials to authenticate a user agent with a server.
Headers.cache-control: The Cache-Control HTTP header field holds directives (instructions) for caching in both requests and responses.
Headers.cf-connecting-ip: A Cloudflare-specific header that identifies the original client IP address of a request passing through the Cloudflare network.
Headers.cf-ray: A Cloudflare-specific header. A unique identifier for every request that passes through Cloudflare, used for troubleshooting.
Headers.connection: The Connection general header controls whether the network connection stays open after the current transaction finishes.
Headers.content-length: The Content-Length header indicates the size of the message body, in bytes, sent to the recipient.
Headers.content-type: The Content-Type representation header is used to indicate the original MIME type of the resource.
Headers.cookie: The Cookie HTTP request header contains stored HTTP cookies previously sent by the server with the Set-Cookie header.
Headers.dnt: The DNT (Do Not Track) request header indicates the user's tracking preference. It lets users opt out of tracking by websites.
Headers.host: The Host request header specifies the host name and port number of the server to which the request is being sent.
Headers.if-none-match: The If-None-Match HTTP request header makes the request conditional. For GET and HEAD methods, the server will return the requested resource only if it doesn't have an ETag matching the given ones.
Headers.origin: The Origin request header indicates where a fetch originates from. It doesn't include any path information, but only the server name.
Headers.priority: The Priority request header is used to indicate the relative priority of a request, used by browsers to optimize resource loading.
Headers.referer: The Referer request header contains the address of the previous web page from which a link to the currently requested page was followed.
Headers.sec-ch-ua: The Sec-CH-UA header provides the user agent's branding and version information via Client Hints.
Headers.sec-ch-ua-mobile: The Sec-CH-UA-Mobile header indicates if the user agent is on a mobile device.
Headers.sec-ch-ua-platform: The Sec-CH-UA-Platform header indicates the platform or operating system on which the user agent is running.
Headers.sec-fetch-dest: The Sec-Fetch-Dest fetch metadata header indicates the request's destination.
Headers.sec-fetch-mode: The Sec-Fetch-Mode fetch metadata header indicates the mode of the request.
Headers.sec-fetch-site: The Sec-Fetch-Site fetch metadata header indicates the relationship between a request initiator's origin and the target resource's origin.
Headers.sec-fetch-user: The Sec-Fetch-User fetch metadata header indicates whether a navigation request was triggered by user activation.
Headers.upgrade-insecure-requests: The Upgrade-Insecure-Requests request header sends a signal to the server expressing the client's preference for an encrypted and authenticated response.
Headers.user-agent: The User-Agent request header is a characteristic string that lets servers and network peers identify the application, operating system, vendor, and/or version of the requesting user agent.
Headers.x-forwarded-for: The X-Forwarded-For (XFF) request header is a de-facto standard header for identifying the originating IP address of a client connecting to a web server through an HTTP proxy or a load balancer.
Headers.x-forwarded-host: The X-Forwarded-Host request header is a de-facto standard header for identifying the original host requested by the client in the Host HTTP request header.
Headers.x-forwarded-proto: The X-Forwarded-Proto request header is a de-facto standard header for identifying the protocol (HTTP or HTTPS) that a client used to connect to your proxy or load balancer.
Headers.x-real-ip: A common de-facto standard header used by proxies and load balancers to pass the original client's IP address to the application server.
Headers.defaultDescription: No detailed description available for this header.

Metadata.title: Headerly - HTTP Request Header Inspector
Metadata.description: Instantly view and inspect your HTTP request headers. A modern, privacy-focused developer tool for debugging browser-server communication.

network.title: Network Details - Headerly
network.description: Explore your connection's technical blueprint. View IP address, location, and ISP details extracted from request metadata.

userAgent.title: User-Agent Analysis - Headerly
userAgent.description: Decode your browser's identity. Understand how browser engines, operating systems, and device types are identified.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy - Headerly
privacy.description: Our commitment to your privacy. Learn how we handle your request data securely and transparently.

terms.title: Terms of Service - Headerly
terms.description: The terms and conditions for using Headerly's developer tools and services.

greenWeb.title: Green Web Analyzer - Headerly
greenWeb.description: Check if a website is hosted on green energy and estimate its carbon footprint.

fingerprint.title: Browser Fingerprint - Headerly
fingerprint.description: Discover your unique digital identifier used for cookie-less tracking.

FingerprintPage.title: Browser Fingerprint
FingerprintPage.subtitle: Discover your unique digital identifier used for cookie-less tracking.

uniqueness.title: Uniqueness Score
uniqueness.description: How identifiable your browser is based on collected data

screen.title: Screen & Color
screen.tooltip: Advertisers use this to know your physical monitor size and infer your device type.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.tooltip: Used to determine your geographical region and show ads when you are most likely awake.

hardware.title: Hardware
hardware.tooltip: Reveals the processing power of your device, distinguishing high-end computers from budget devices.

platform.title: Platform
platform.tooltip: Identifies your operating system (Windows, macOS, Linux, etc.) to serve targeted software.

language.title: Language
language.tooltip: Your browser's primary language helps identify your nationality or cultural background.

cookies.title: Cookies Enabled
cookies.tooltip: If disabled, sites will aggressively use fingerprinting instead of traditional tracking.

dnt.title: Do Not Track
dnt.tooltip: Ironically, having 'Do Not Track' enabled makes you MORE unique, as few users turn it on.

touch.title: Touch Support
touch.tooltip: Instantly separates mobile/tablet devices from traditional desktop computers.

pixelRatio.title: Pixel Ratio
pixelRatio.tooltip: Reveals your system's zoom level or whether you have a Retina/high-DPI display.

canvas.title: Canvas Fingerprint
canvas.tooltip: Draws invisible graphics to extract microscopic differences in how your GPU renders pixels.

webgl.title: WebGL Context
webgl.tooltip: Directly exposes the exact model of your Graphics Card (GPU) and its drivers.

howItWorks.title: How does this work?
howItWorks.subtitle: The mechanics of cookie-less tracking.
howItWorks.p1: Even when you block cookies or use Incognito mode, your browser shares a unique set of hardware and software configurations with websites you visit. Scripts can silently gather this data to create a 'fingerprint' that uniquely identifies your device across the web.

cookieless.title: Cookie-less Tracking
cookieless.description: Tracks you across sessions even in Incognito mode or with cookies disabled.

canvasRender.title: Hardware Canvas
canvasRender.description: Rendering invisible graphics reveals the specific nuances of your GPU and drivers.

privacy.title: Privacy Measures
privacy.description: Browsers like Brave, Tor, or privacy extensions can randomize these APIs to mitigate tracking.

stateless.title: Stateless Check
stateless.description: We generate this hash entirely on your client. It is never sent to or stored on our servers.

actionableSteps.title: How to Protect Yourself
actionableSteps.subtitle: Actionable steps to reduce your digital footprint.

browsers.title: Use Privacy-Focused Browsers
browsers.description: Browsers like Brave, Tor, or Firefox (with Strict Tracking Protection) actively randomize or block fingerprinting APIs.

extensions.title: Install Anti-Tracking Extensions
extensions.description: Extensions like uBlock Origin or Privacy Badger block known tracking scripts before they can execute and build your profile.

incognito.title: Understand Incognito Limits
incognito.description: Remember that Incognito/Private mode only clears your local history and cookies. It does NOT hide your browser fingerprint.

settings.title: Disable Unnecessary Features
settings.description: Disabling WebGL or third-party cookies in your browser settings can reduce the surface area available to trackers.
